title: Casselia zelota
uses: Casselia zelota has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Studies have also shown that Casselia zelota contains compounds with potential antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in folk medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues and reduce inflammation. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.
taxonomy: Casselia zelota is a flowering plant species within the family Rutaceae. This genus is also known as Casselia, which belongs to the order Sapindales. The plant's classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Further classification includes the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. Casselia zelota's classification is also expressed using the binomial nomenclature system, with the species name 'zelota'.

intro: Casselia zelota, also known as Brazilian cherry or cipo, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Casselia zelota include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Casselia zelota include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
